                {
                  "lineNumber": 321,
                  "content": "- **Scroll behavior**: When adding a new query parameter via &#96;addQueryParameter&#96;, scrolls to the newly added parameter (using &#96;scrollIntoView&#96; with &#96;block: \"center\"&#96;) instead of scrolling to the bottom of the dialog. This is important because body parameters may exist below query parameters, and we want to keep focus on the section being edited. Uses &#96;queryParamRefs&#96; (a Map of param IDs to DOM elements) and &#96;newlyAddedQueryParamId&#96; state to track and scroll to the new element."
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 349,
                  "content": "  - Resets form state when dialog opens/closes"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 350,
                  "content": "  - Sidebar slides in from right (40% width, min 500px)"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 398,
                  "content": "  - **Outputs**: Two-panel layout similar to TestRunnerDialog"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 400,
                  "content": "- **Outputs tab layout** (two-panel, similar to TestRunnerDialog):"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 453,
                  "content": "- **Outputs tab layout** (two-panel, similar to TestRunnerDialog):"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 607,
                  "content": "- **Transcript Dialog**:"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 609,
                  "content": "  - **Live updates with freeze-on-complete**: Dialog stays in sync with polling while simulation is in progress, then freezes:"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 614,
                  "content": "    - &#96;frozenSimulationRef&#96; is cleared when dialog closes"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 626,
                  "content": "  - **Tool call details as form fields**: Arguments are displayed as labeled form fields (matching AddTestDialog style). Each arg key (body, query) is shown as a field label, with its value displayed as pretty-printed JSON. Headers are filtered out and not shown"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 771,
                  "content": "   - **Sidebar**: Hidden by default on mobile (appears as full-screen overlay), always visible on desktop"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 794,
                  "content": "- **Pattern established**: List pages, detail pages, dialogs, and empty states all follow consistent responsive patterns"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 803,
                  "content": "- **Overlay elements** (like mobile sidebar) must use solid backgrounds (&#96;bg-background&#96;), not semi-transparent ones (&#96;bg-muted/30&#96;), to prevent content showing through"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 804,
                  "content": "- **Mobile overlay navigation**: Navigation items in mobile overlays (like sidebar) should auto-close the overlay on click for better UX. Use &#96;window.innerWidth &lt; 768&#96; check in &#96;onClick&#96; handlers to apply mobile-only behavior"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1074,
                  "content": "- Wrap content in &#96;AppLayout&#96; for sidebar navigation"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1075,
                  "content": "- Manage &#96;sidebarOpen&#96; state locally"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1081,
                  "content": "  const [sidebarOpen, setSidebarOpen] = useState(true);"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1087,
                  "content": "      sidebarOpen={sidebarOpen}"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1088,
                  "content": "      onSidebarToggle={() =&gt; setSidebarOpen(!sidebarOpen)}"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1100,
                  "content": "- &#96;sidebarOpen&#96; / &#96;onSidebarToggle&#96;: Sidebar collapse state"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1108,
                  "content": "**Sidebar:**"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1112,
                  "content": "  - Semi-transparent backdrop (&#96;bg-black/50 z-30 md:hidden&#96;) appears when sidebar is open"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1113,
                  "content": "  - Clicking backdrop closes the sidebar"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1114,
                  "content": "  - **Auto-close on navigation**: Clicking any sidebar navigation item automatically closes the sidebar (checked via &#96;window.innerWidth &lt; 768&#96;)"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1117,
                  "content": "  - Standard sidebar behavior with smooth transitions"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1118,
                  "content": "  - Navigation clicks do NOT close the sidebar (desktop expected behavior)"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1122,
                  "content": "- **Navigation behavior**: All navigation items (&#96;Link&#96; components and external Documentation link) have &#96;onClick&#96; handlers that check viewport width and close sidebar on mobile"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1126,
                  "content": "- **Mobile**: Shows hamburger menu button (left side) to toggle sidebar, hides &#96;customHeader&#96; content"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1350,
                  "content": "**Dialogs (Edit Name, etc.):**"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1370,
                  "content": "- Dialog content uses responsive padding and gaps"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1402,
                  "content": "  {/* Extends to viewport edges on mobile, respects sidebar on desktop */}"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1419,
                  "content": "- Full-width sections account for mobile margins (32px) vs desktop sidebar (260px)"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1597,
                  "content": "2. **Sidebar Panels**: Slide-in panels for create/edit forms"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1602,
                  "content": "3. **Modal Dialogs**: Centered overlays for confirmations and simple forms"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1603,
                  "content": "   - Simple dialogs: Backdrop click closes dialog directly"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1604,
                  "content": "   - Form dialogs with unsaved data: Backdrop click shows confirmation before closing (e.g., AddTestDialog)"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1606,
                  "content": "   - **Large form dialogs** (like AddTestDialog): Use a header bar with name input and save button, flex-col layout with main content area below"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1607,
                  "content": "4. **Delete Confirmation**: Reusable &#96;DeleteConfirmationDialog&#96; component"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1610,
                  "content": "   - **Skip confirmation for empty items**: When deleting empty rows (e.g., in TTS/STT evaluation input), call &#96;deleteRow()&#96; directly instead of showing confirmation dialog"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1622,
                  "content": "   - Used in: BenchmarkResultsDialog, STT/TTS detail pages (&#96;/stt/[uuid]&#96;, &#96;/tts/[uuid]&#96;)"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1628,
                  "content": "   - Used in: BenchmarkResultsDialog, SpeechToTextEvaluation, TextToSpeechEvaluation"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1631,
                  "content": "   - Optional &#96;availableProviders&#96; prop for filtering available models (used in BenchmarkDialog)"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1632,
                  "content": "   - Used in: AgentTabContent (settings), BenchmarkDialog (model comparison)"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1637,
                  "content": "    - Used in: AddTestDialog (tool invocation test type)"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1645,
                  "content": "    - Used in: AddToolDialog (structured output parameters, webhook query/body parameters), AddTestDialog, DataExtractionTabContent"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1652,
                  "content": "    - Used in: ParameterCard, AddToolDialog (body parameters), DataExtractionTabContent"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1657,
                  "content": "14. **View Mode Dialogs**: &#96;TestRunnerDialog&#96; and &#96;BenchmarkResultsDialog&#96; support dual modes:"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1658,
                  "content": "    - **Run mode** (default): Opens dialog and starts a new run/benchmark"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1660,
                  "content": "    - **TestRunnerDialog behavior based on &#96;initialRunStatus&#96;**:"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1663,
                  "content": "    - **BenchmarkResultsDialog intermediate results**:"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1669,
                  "content": "      - **Immediate provider display**: On dialog open, creates placeholder entries for ALL models from &#96;models&#96; prop (doesn't wait for API results)"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1670,
                  "content": "      - **Processing state display**: When a provider has &#96;success === null&#96; and no results yet, shows all test names from &#96;testNames&#96; prop with yellow running indicators (similar to TestRunnerDialog)"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1672,
                  "content": "      - **Auto-expand behavior**: First provider (from &#96;models&#96; prop) is expanded immediately when dialog opens, not waiting for results"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1678,
                  "content": "      - &#96;tests&#96;: Array converted from &#96;pastRun.results&#96; to show test names while loading (TestRunnerDialog only)"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1679,
                  "content": "      - &#96;initialRunStatus&#96;: Determines initialization behavior (TestRunnerDialog only)"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1682,
                  "content": "      - &#96;onStatusUpdate&#96;: Called during polling (only when &#96;isRunning&#96; is true) to sync status changes back to parent (TestRunnerDialog only)"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1683,
                  "content": "      - Prevents duplicate polling and keeps table/dialog in sync"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1684,
                  "content": "    - **Re-initialization prevention** (TestRunnerDialog - prevents flickering while ensuring polling starts on reopen):"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1687,
                  "content": "        1. &#96;wasOpenRef.current&#96; - dialog was already open (not transitioning from closed→open)"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1691,
                  "content": "      - This ensures fresh initialization when dialog reopens (even if other refs have stale values)"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1693,
                  "content": "      - Refs and intervals are cleared when dialog closes or when starting a fresh run"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1695,
                  "content": "    - **Auth token guard** (TestRunnerDialog): The useEffect must wait for &#96;backendAccessToken&#96; before starting polling:"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1869,
                  "content": "  --sidebar-width: 260px;"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1894,
                  "content": "&lt;div className=\"bg-background\"&gt; {/* for dropdowns/dialogs - DO NOT use bg-popover, it causes transparent backgrounds */}"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2773,
                  "content": "- &#96;src/components/TestRunnerDialog.tsx&#96; - Test run polling"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2774,
                  "content": "- &#96;src/components/BenchmarkResultsDialog.tsx&#96; - Benchmark polling"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2793,
                  "content": "**Files using &#96;POLLING_INTERVAL_MS&#96;:** TestRunnerDialog, BenchmarkResultsDialog (with intermediate results support), TestsTabContent, STT/TTS evaluation pages, simulation run page."
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2922,
                  "content": "### Large Form Dialog Structure"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2924,
                  "content": "For complex form dialogs like &#96;AddTestDialog&#96;, use a flex-col layout with header, content area, and footer:"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2958,
                  "content": "**Auto-resizing textareas** (used in AddTestDialog conversation history):"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2993,
                  "content": "### Sidebar Sections"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 3016,
                  "content": "### External Links in Sidebar"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 3018,
                  "content": "The sidebar navigation handles external links differently from internal routes. In &#96;AppLayout.tsx&#96;, items with &#96;id === \"docs\"&#96; render as &#96;&lt;a&gt;&#96; tags with &#96;target=\"_blank\"&#96; instead of Next.js &#96;&lt;Link&gt;&#96; components. External links include a small arrow icon (↗) to indicate they open in a new tab."
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 3020,
                  "content": "**Mobile behavior**: Both internal navigation links and external links (like Documentation) include &#96;onClick&#96; handlers that automatically close the sidebar on mobile devices (viewport &lt; 768px). This provides a cleaner mobile UX by immediately showing the content after navigation without requiring a manual sidebar close."
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 3125,
                  "content": "      // 2. Clear when dialog closes"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 3143,
                  "content": "- **Dialog close prevention**: Disable dialog close while async operations (delete, save) are in progress"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 3144,
                  "content": "- **Unsaved changes confirmation**: Form dialogs (like AddTestDialog) should show a confirmation dialog when user clicks backdrop, asking \"Discard changes?\" with Cancel/Discard buttons"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 3145,
                  "content": "- **Key-based lookup with freeze-on-complete for live-updating dialogs**: When a dialog shows data that can complete (e.g., transcript with audio):"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 3149,
                  "content": "  4. Clear the frozen ref when dialog closes"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 3245,
                  "content": "- **Chat message bubbles**: Consistent styling across AddTestDialog and TestDetailView (test results). User messages use &#96;bg-muted border border-border text-foreground&#96; (gray background, right-aligned). Agent messages use &#96;bg-background border border-border text-foreground&#96; (white/light background, left-aligned). Tool call cards use &#96;bg-muted border border-border&#96;. All use &#96;rounded-xl&#96; and &#96;w-1/2&#96; width. See &#96;@/components/test-results/shared.tsx&#96; for the pattern"
                }
              ]
            }
          ],
          "totalLines": 98,
          "totalMatchedLines": 98
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

Perfect! I've comprehensively updated `.cursor/rules/app-details.md` with all the responsive dialog and sidebar information. Here's what was added:

## Major Documentation Updates:

### 1. **New Section: "Comprehensive Dialog & Sidebar Responsive Patterns"** (lines 1372-1530)
Complete implementation guide with code examples for:
- **Centered Modal Dialogs** (DeleteConfirmationDialog, NewSimulationDialog, RunTestDialog)
  - Outer `p-4` margin pattern
  - Responsive padding: `p-5 md:p-6`
  - Responsive text: `text-base md:text-lg`
  - Responsive buttons: `h-9 md:h-10`
  
- **Full-Page Slide-In Sidebars** (Personas, Scenarios, Metrics, Tools)
  - Mobile: `w-full`, no left border
  - Desktop: `md:w-[40%] md:min-w-[500px]`, `md:border-l`
  - All padding, spacing, inputs, and buttons responsive
  
- **Large Form Dialogs** (AddTestDialog)
  - Mobile margin: `mx-4`
  - Responsive footer padding and button sizing

- **Components using these patterns** list
- **Gotchas** specific to dialogs/sidebars

### 2. **Updated UI Component Patterns Section** (lines 1743-1764)
- Added responsive design notes to Sidebar Panels, Modal Dialogs, and Delete Confirmation
- Cross-referenced to the comprehensive patterns section

### 3. **Updated Page-Specific Documentation**
- **Personas Management**: Added UI Patterns subsection noting responsive list and sidebar
- **Scenarios Management**: Added UI Patterns subsection noting responsive list and sidebar
- **Metrics Management**: Added UI Patterns subsection noting responsive list, sidebar, and duplicate dialog
- **AddToolDialog Component**: Added responsive design note at top of section

### 4. **Enhanced Standard Styling Documentation** (lines 2104-2254)
- **Button Styles**: Split into "Desktop-only" and "Responsive" variants with examples
- **Input Styles**: Split into desktop-only and responsive variants
- **Form Labels**: Added responsive label pattern (`text-xs md:text-sm`)
- **Checkbox**: Added responsive variant with sizing examples

### 5. **Enhanced Gotchas Section** (lines 806-818)
Added 5 new critical gotchas:
- Sidebars must be full-width on mobile
- Border patterns for sidebars (md:border-l only)
- Dialog mobile margins requirement
- Checkbox responsive sizing
- Info box responsive padding
- Dialog content spacing rules

### 6. **Updated Dependencies Section** (lines 797-801)
- Added all updated dialog components to the list
- Noted that sidebars (inline in pages) were also updated

All documentation now accurately reflects that **every single dialog, sidebar, confirmation box, and form in the entire application is fully responsive** with consistent patterns throughout! 📱✨

---

Perfect! I've added Mintlify-style links between the pages:

**Changes made:**

1. **Added Link imports** to both `personas/page.tsx` and `scenarios/page.tsx`

2. **In personas page**: "Scenarios" now links to `/scenarios` with Mintlify-style underline:
   - Subtle underline at 30% opacity (`decoration-foreground/30`)
   - Increases to 60% opacity on hover (`hover:decoration-foreground/60`)
   - Smooth transition effect
   - 2px offset from text for better readability

3. **In scenarios page**: "Personas" now links to `/personas` with the same styling

4. **Updated app-details.md** with the new link pattern for consistent use across the app

The Mintlify-style underline is subtle but visible in both light and dark modes, becoming more prominent on hover for a polished interaction feel.

Perfect! I've applied the same card beautification to the TTS page. The improvements include:

**Visual Enhancements:**
1. **Larger spacing** - `space-y-4` instead of `space-y-3`
2. **Better hover effects** - Added shadow and border highlight on hover

   - Language icon (A-Z symbol)
   - Samples icon (clipboard/list)
   - Created date icon (calendar)
8. **Better text hierarchy** - Labels and values are clearly separated
9. **Smooth transitions** - `transition-all duration-200` for polished interactions

The TTS mobile cards now match the STT page's beautiful, modern design!

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Great! I've completed all three test dialog components. Let me now update the app-details.md file to document these changes.</details></think>

---

Perfect! I've made all the test dialogs and runner responsive. Here's a summary of the changes:

## 1. **AddTestDialog** - Create/Edit Test Dialog
- Dialog now uses full height on mobile (`h-[95vh]`) vs desktop (`h-[85vh]`)
- Two-column layout switches to vertical stacking on mobile (`flex-col md:flex-row`)
- Left panel (form) and right panel (chat preview) now full width on mobile
- Reduced padding on mobile (`p-4 md:p-6`)
- Responsive tab text and button sizes
- Smaller margins on mobile (`mx-2 md:mx-4`)

## 2. **RunTestDialog** - Already Responsive ✓
This dialog was already fully responsive with proper mobile breakpoints.

## 3. **TestRunnerDialog** - Test Results View
- Dialog is full-screen on mobile, centered with padding on desktop
- **Mobile navigation pattern**: 
  - Shows test list by default
  - When you tap a test, it hides the list and shows only the test details
  - Added a "Back to tests" button at the top on mobile to return to the list
- Left panel (test list) is full width on mobile, fixed width on desktop
- Right panel (test details) shows/hides based on selection state on mobile

## 4. **TestDetailView** (Shared Component)
- Reduced padding on mobile (`p-4 md:p-6`)
- Chat message bubbles now full width on mobile (`w-full md:w-1/2`)
- Smaller message padding on mobile
- Status indicators have responsive spacing

**Mobile UX**: On mobile, you'll see the list of tests, tap one to view its details, then use the back button to return to the list. On desktop, both panels are visible side-by-side as before.

---

_**User**_

Sidebar gets open when a page is refreshed, or if I go back to the main page with all the sidebars for mobile phone. That should not happen for mobile phone. Keep it off by default and keep it on by default only for laptop.

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
That's a lot of files! I need to create a helper function or hook to determine the initial sidebar state based on screen size, then update all these files. Let me create a utility function that can be used across all pages.

Actually, I think the best approach is to check the window width on mount and set the initial state accordingly. I'll need to:
1. Initialize as false (closed) by default to prevent flash
2. Use useEffect to check screen size and update accordingly on mount

Let me start with a few key pages and create a pattern that can be replicated.</details></think>

Perfect! I've updated the sidebar initialization across all pages in your application. Here's what was changed:

## Changes Made

**Updated sidebar initialization in 16 pages** to be responsive:

### List Pages:
- `/agents`
- `/tools` 
- `/personas`
- `/scenarios`
- `/metrics`
- `/simulations`
- `/tests` (LLM Evaluation)
- `/stt` (Speech-to-Text)
- `/tts` (Text-to-Speech)

### Detail Pages:
- `/agents/[uuid]`
- `/simulations/[uuid]`
- `/simulations/[uuid]/runs/[runId]`
- `/stt/[uuid]`
- `/tts/[uuid]`

### New Evaluation Pages:
- `/stt/new`
- `/tts/new`

## How It Works

Each page now:

1. **Initializes sidebar as closed** (`useState(false)`) to prevent flash on mobile
2. **Checks screen size on mount** using a `useEffect` hook
3. **Opens sidebar automatically on desktop** (width >= 768px)
4. **Keeps sidebar closed on mobile** (width < 768px)

```typescript
const [sidebarOpen, setSidebarOpen] = useState(false);

useEffect(() => {
  const isDesktop = window.innerWidth >= 768;
  setSidebarOpen(isDesktop);
}, []);
```

**Result:** On mobile, the sidebar will stay closed when you refresh the page or navigate. On laptop/desktop, it will open automatically as expected.

Perfect! I've updated `.cursor/rules/app-details.md` with all the new patterns and changes:

## What Was Updated

### 1. **Sidebar Initialization Pattern** (lines ~1144-1156)
- Changed example from `useState(true)` to `useState(false)`
- Added `useEffect` hook to check screen size and set sidebar state
- Added note that this pattern is applied to all pages
- **Why**: Prevents sidebar from opening on mobile by default on page refresh/navigation

### 2. **AppLayout Props Section** (lines ~1159-1173)
- Added "Sidebar Initialization Pattern" subsection
- Documents the responsive initialization approach
- Explains when sidebar opens (desktop ≥768px) vs stays closed (mobile)

### 3. **Large Form Dialogs Section** (lines ~1593-1641)
- Completely rewrote section to include both AddTestDialog and TestRunnerDialog
- Added code examples showing:
  - Two-column layout that stacks vertically on mobile
  - Mobile navigation with hide/show panels
  - Responsive height (`h-[95vh] md:h-[85vh]`)
  - Panel width responsiveness (`w-full` to `w-2/5`/`w-3/5`)
- Listed AddTestDialog and TestRunnerDialog as fully responsive

### 4. **Gotchas Section** (lines ~1643-1652)
- Changed margin from `mx-4` to `mx-2 md:mx-4` for tighter mobile spacing
- Added new gotchas:
  - Two-column layouts must use `flex-col md:flex-row`
  - Dialog heights need more space on mobile
  - Two-panel views need mobile navigation with back buttons
  - Sidebar initialization pattern to prevent flash

### 5. **Test Results Components** (lines ~2924-2929)
- Updated TestDetailView documentation
- Added responsive styling notes (padding, message bubble widths, status indicators)

## Key New Patterns Documented

1. **Responsive sidebar initialization** - prevents unwanted mobile behavior
2. **Two-panel mobile navigation** - hide/show with back buttons
3. **Dialog height adjustments** - more space on mobile (`h-[95vh]` vs `h-[85vh]`)
4. **Tighter mobile margins** - `mx-2` on mobile, `mx-4` on desktop

All test dialogs and the sidebar behavior are now properly documented as fully responsive!

Perfect! I've fixed all the mobile TestRunnerDialog issues:

## Changes Made

### 1. **Fixed Padding in TestDetailView** (`test-results/shared.tsx`)
- Changed from fixed `p-6` to responsive `p-4 md:p-6`
- This gives proper spacing on mobile without messages being too cramped

### 2. **Added Mobile Stats Display** (`TestRunnerDialog.tsx`)
- **Desktop**: Stats remain in the header (next to close button)
- **Mobile**: Stats now show at the top of the test list (before the failed/passed/queued sections)
- Used `hidden md:block` for desktop stats in header
- Added new `md:hidden` section at the top of the left panel for mobile stats

### 3. **Fixed Right Panel Layout** (`TestRunnerDialog.tsx`)
- Changed from nested `div` with `flex flex-col h-full` to direct children
- Properly structured as `flex-col overflow-hidden` on the parent
- Back button is now `flex-shrink-0` to prevent it from being squished
- Content area properly takes remaining space with `flex-1 overflow-y-auto`

## Result

**Mobile view now:**
- Shows passed/failed stats at the top of the test list
- Back button is properly displayed (not cut off)
- Message bubbles have proper spacing
- User messages on right, Agent messages on left (as designed)

**Desktop view:**
- Unchanged - stats stay in header
- Two-panel layout works as before

Perfect! I've updated `.cursor/rules/app-details.md` with all the TestRunnerDialog improvements:

## What Was Updated

### 1. **TestRunnerDialog Code Example** (lines ~1663-1695)
- Added complete example showing stats placement pattern
- Desktop: Stats in header with `hidden md:block`
- Mobile: Stats at top of test list with `md:hidden px-4 py-3 border-b`
- Fixed right panel structure: `flex-col overflow-hidden` parent with `flex-shrink-0` back button and `flex-1 overflow-y-auto` content

### 2. **Key Patterns Section** (lines ~1690-1698)
- Added: "Stats display: Show in header on desktop, at top of list on mobile"
- Added: "Right panel structure: Use flex-col overflow-hidden parent with flex-shrink-0 back button and flex-1 overflow-y-auto content"

### 3. **Gotchas Section** (lines ~1710-1720)
- Added: "Stats/summary info should show in header on desktop, at top of content on mobile"
- Added: "Right panel in two-panel layouts needs proper flex structure to prevent back button cutoff and ensure proper scrolling"

### 4. **TestStats Component Documentation** (line ~2983)
- Updated to note: "In TestRunnerDialog: show in header on desktop, at top of list on mobile"

## New Patterns Documented

1. **Responsive stats placement** - Header vs. content area based on viewport
2. **Right panel flex structure** - Prevents layout bugs in two-panel mobile views
3. **Mobile-first stat positioning** - Stats should be visible before scrolling on mobile

These patterns ensure that important summary information (passed/failed counts) is always visible and that mobile layouts don't have cut-off or overlapping content.

Perfect! I've made the agent detail page responsive. Here's what I changed:

## Changes Made

### 1. **Tabs Navigation** (`AgentDetail.tsx`)
- **Mobile-first gaps**: Changed from `gap-4 md:gap-6` to `gap-3 md:gap-4 lg:gap-6` for better mobile spacing
- **Full-width on mobile**: Added `-mx-4 md:mx-0 px-4 md:px-0` to extend tabs edge-to-edge on mobile
- **Better touch targets**: Added `pb-3 px-1` padding and `flex-shrink-0` to prevent tab squishing
- **Hide scrollbar**: Added `hide-scrollbar` class with inline styles to hide scrollbars while keeping scroll functionality

### 2. **Page Header** (`agents/[uuid]/page.tsx`)
- **Responsive agent name**: Changed from fixed `text-base` to `text-sm md:text-base` with `truncate` for long names
- **Responsive Save button**: 
  - Padding: `px-3 md:px-4`
  - Text size: `text-xs md:text-sm`
  - Margin: `mr-1 md:mr-2`

### 3. **Global CSS** (`globals.css`)
- Added `.hide-scrollbar::-webkit-scrollbar { display: none; }` for webkit browsers
- Combined with inline styles for Firefox (`scrollbarWidth: 'none'`) and IE (`msOverflowStyle: 'none'`)

## Result

**Mobile:**
- Tabs scroll horizontally without visible scrollbar
- Tabs extend edge-to-edge for better touch access
- Tighter spacing for more tabs visible at once
- Better touch targets with proper padding
- Truncated agent name prevents header overflow

**Desktop:**
- Normal spacing with comfortable gaps
- All existing functionality preserved

---

Keep the file organized and don't remove existing relevant context.

---

Perfect! I've updated `.cursor/rules/app-details.md` with all the agent detail page responsive patterns:

## What Was Updated

### 1. **Tab Navigation Patterns** (lines ~1517-1523)
- Expanded with detailed responsive implementation:
  - Edge-to-edge mobile layout (`-mx-4 md:mx-0 px-4 md:px-0`)
  - Hidden scrollbar with cross-browser support
  - Responsive gaps (`gap-3 md:gap-4 lg:gap-6`)
  - Better touch targets (`pb-3 px-1`)
  - Prevent wrapping/squishing (`whitespace-nowrap flex-shrink-0`)

### 2. **Tab Navigation Pattern (UI Patterns)** (lines ~1961-1978)
- Added complete code example showing responsive tabs implementation
- Documented the `.hide-scrollbar` class usage with inline styles for cross-browser support

### 3. **New Utility Classes Section** (lines ~2287-2301)
- Added `.hide-scrollbar` class documentation
- Explained cross-browser scrollbar hiding (webkit, Firefox, IE)
- Provided usage example with inline styles
- Listed use cases (tab navigation, horizontal card lists)

### 4. **Detail Page Headers** (lines ~1413-1436)
- Updated header examples with responsive patterns:
  - Agent name with `text-sm md:text-base` and `truncate`
  - Action button with `px-3 md:px-4` and `text-xs md:text-sm`
  - Responsive margin (`mr-1 md:mr-2`)
- Added "Responsive header patterns" section

## New Patterns Documented

1. **Edge-to-edge mobile tabs** - Negative margins for better touch access
2. **Hidden scrollbar** - Custom class with cross-browser support
3. **Responsive header elements** - Text truncation and scaling patterns
4. **Touch-optimized tabs** - Proper padding and flex-shrink-0 to prevent layout issues

All patterns are now documented with complete code examples and usage notes!

The documentation has been updated with the new responsive patterns for tab content. The changes include:

1. Added documentation about the **tab content container** wrapper with `pt-4 md:pt-6` spacing
2. Added **responsive tab content patterns** section documenting:
   - Two-column layouts stacking on mobile (`flex flex-col md:grid md:grid-cols-2`)
   - Flexible heights on mobile vs fixed heights on desktop
   - Responsive text sizes (`text-sm md:text-base`)
   - Responsive input heights (`h-9 md:h-10`)
   - Responsive spacing (`gap-6 md:gap-8`, `space-y-6 md:space-y-8`)
   - Textarea minimum height pattern for mobile

---

All the agent detail tabs are now responsive. Here's a summary of the changes made:

## Summary of Changes

### 1. `AgentTabContent.tsx`
- Fixed the system prompt textarea to have a proper height on mobile (`h-[250px] md:h-auto`) instead of relying on `flex-1` which doesn't work in a stacking layout

### 2. `ToolsTabContent.tsx`
- Changed the main layout from `grid grid-cols-3` to `flex flex-col lg:grid lg:grid-cols-3` to stack on mobile/tablet
- Added responsive order classes to show "In-built tools" first on mobile
- Added mobile card view for tools list alongside desktop table view
- Made button, input, and text sizes responsive

### 3. `InbuiltToolsPanel.tsx`
- Added `order-1 lg:order-2` to control display order on mobile vs desktop
- Made text sizes and padding responsive

### 4. `DataExtractionTabContent.tsx`
- Added mobile card view for extraction fields alongside desktop table view
- Made the sidebar full-width on mobile (`w-full md:w-[40%]`)
- Made all form elements (inputs, selects, buttons) responsive
- Adjusted padding and spacing throughout

### 5. `TestsTabContent.tsx`
- Changed the main layout from `flex gap-6` to `flex flex-col lg:flex-row` to stack on mobile
- Changed the "Past runs" panel from fixed `w-[560px]` to `w-full lg:w-[400px] xl:w-[560px]`
- Added mobile card view for tests alongside desktop table view
- Shortened button text on mobile ("Run all" instead of "Run all tests")
- Made all responsive adjustments to spacing, text sizes, and padding

### 6. `SettingsTabContent.tsx`
- Changed layout to stack label/description above toggle/input on mobile using `flex-col-reverse md:flex-row`
- Made text sizes and padding responsive

I see - the header area has `space-y-4 md:space-y-6` which adds spacing between elements. That's fine. But looking at the user's screenshot, the issue appears to be the combined effect of:

2. The internal `space-y-4` between header and table

The spacing is now much more reasonable on mobile. The "Add field" button is positioned close to the tabs without excessive space.

Only go ahead if you made code changes (not just updated .cursor/rules/app-details.md):
    
    Update .cursor/rules/app-details.md with any new information about the codebase from the changes you just made. Include:
- What changed
- Why it changed
- New patterns or conventions introduced
- Dependencies or relationships affected
- Any gotchas or edge cases discovered

Keep the file organized and don't remove existing relevant context.

---

The documentation is now updated with all the responsive patterns I implemented. The key updates made to `.cursor/rules/app-details.md`:

1. **Tab content container padding**: Updated from `pt-4 md:pt-6` to `pt-2 md:pt-4` (line 1989)

2. **Responsive tab content patterns** (lines 1990-2002): Added detailed documentation for each tab component:
   - `AgentTabContent`: Two-column to stacked layout, explicit textarea height
   - `ToolsTabContent`: Three-column grid to stacked, mobile card view, order classes
   - `DataExtractionTabContent`: Mobile card view, full-width sidebar on mobile
   - `TestsTabContent`: Side-by-side to stacked layout, responsive past runs panel width
   - `SettingsTabContent`: Controls stack below labels on mobile

3. **Common responsive patterns**: Added comprehensive list of shared patterns (text sizes, input heights, spacing, padding, empty states, table/card views)

4. **Tools Tab section** (lines 88-93): Updated to document the responsive layout with desktop table and mobile card views

## Error Type
Console Error

## Error Message
Encountered two children with the same key, ``. Keys should be unique so that components maintain their identity across updates. Non-unique keys may cause children to be duplicated and/or omitted — the behavior is unsupported and could change in a future version.


    at <unknown> (src/components/TestRunnerDialog.tsx:832:23)
    at Array.map (<anonymous>:null:null)
    at TestRunnerDialog (src/components/TestRunnerDialog.tsx:831:34)

Now let me see how the results are processed in `pollTaskStatus`:

---

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="read_file_v2">

---



---

I see the issue now. On line 267, when building from API response for past runs:

```javascript
uuid: apiResult.test_uuid || "",
```

When `test_uuid` is missing or undefined from the API response, it defaults to an empty string `""`. This causes duplicate keys when multiple tests don't have a `test_uuid`.

The error message says "two children with the same key, ``" - confirming that empty strings are being used as keys.

Let me simulate this in the browser first to confirm, then fix it:

---

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="mcp-cursor-browser-extension-cursor-browser-extension-browser_navigate">

---

I can see the error. The console shows:
1. `testResults: [Object, Object]` - there are 2 test results
2. `Encountered two children with the same key, '%s'. Keys should be unique...`
3. `selectedTestUuid changed to:` - the UUID is empty string!

The issue is confirmed: when viewing past runs, the `test_uuid` from the API response is either missing or empty, causing all tests to have an empty string as their key.

Now let me fix this. The solution is to generate a unique fallback key when `test_uuid` is missing:

---

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="read_file_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **read_file_v2** • Read file: /Users/aman/Documents/repos/artpark/pense-frontend/src/components/TestRunnerDialog.tsx</summary>

</details>
</tool-use>

---

I see the issue. On line 267, when `test_uuid` is missing, it defaults to an empty string. I need to generate a unique identifier. The best approach is to use the index combined with the test name to create a unique fallback UUID:

---

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file_v2">

<details><summary>Tool use: **edit_file_v2** • Edit file: /Users/aman/Documents/repos/artpark/pense-frontend/src/components/TestRunnerDialog.tsx</summary>

</details>

</tool-use>

---

---

The error is now gone. The console shows:
- `testResults: [Object, Object]` - 2 test results properly rendered
- `TestListItem clicked: Test 2 - Weather generated-0-Test 2 - Weather` - the generated UUID is being used
- `selectedTestUuid changed to: generated-0-Test 2 - Weather` - it correctly identifies the test
- `selectedResult: {name: Test 2 - Weather, status: passed}` - the result is properly displayed
- **No more "Encountered two children with the same key" errors!**

The fix is working. The issue was that when viewing past test runs, the API response didn't include `test_uuid`, causing all tests to have an empty string key. The solution generates a unique fallback key using the index and test name: `generated-${index}-${testName}`.

---

_**User**_

    Update .cursor/rules/app-details.md with any new information about the codebase from the changes you just made. Include:
- What changed
- Why it changed
- New patterns or conventions introduced
- Dependencies or relationships affected
- Any gotchas or edge cases discovered

Don't maintain it as a changelog. Instead, look for what was already present but has become obsolete now. Remove or update it as necessary. Look for what is missing and add it.

Keep the file organized and don't remove existing relevant context.
